paragraph: The men's 200-metre backstroke at the Navros Aquatic Classic on April 8 was defined by the wire-to-wire front-running of Elro Kend of the Navros Swimming Programme, who set a conservative turn at 50 metres in 27.88 before progressively accelerating on each subsequent leg to finish in 1:56.47 — a national record by 0.33 seconds. paragraph: Platform diving's men's 10-metre individual final at the Mervantian Aquatic Championship on June 17 ended in controversy when competition leader Kemnath of the Vostral Diving Federation was assessed a failed-dive ruling on his fifth attempt — a back 3.5 somersault in tuck — after one of the five judges activated the failed-dive protocol upon observing the athlete's feet make contact with the 10-metre platform during the run-up phase.
